visa_types.name: Long Term Visa (More than 90 days)
visa_types.overview: Anyone who remains in Switzerland for longer than three months will need to apply for a long term visa. The visa application for a long-term visa has to be submitted directly at the Swiss Embassy, Qatar. For more information  click here (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/qatar/en/home/visa/entry-ch.html).
visa_types.full_text: Anyone who remains in Switzerland for longer than three months will need to apply for a long term visa. The visa application for a long-term visa has to be submitted directly at the Swiss Embassy, Qatar. For more information  click here (https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/qatar/en/home/visa/entry-ch.html).

visa_types.name: Student
visa_types.documents_required: General requirements for applicants applying for Student visaPlease contact the Embassy of Switzerland in Qatar by mail (doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch (mailto:doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch)) in order to receive the information on the documentation to submit in order to apply for a “Student visa”.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Please contact the Swiss Embassy in Qatar directly in order to fix an appointment to submit your application form for a student visa.
Visa Fee: No Visa fees is applicable for applicants applying under student category.
visa_types.processing_time: Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid for study purpose in Switzerland only.
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa. It can only be submitted at the Swiss Embassy in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to submit their complete application in person at the Swiss Embassy, Qatar. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid for study purpose in Switzerland only.
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa. It can only be submitted at the Swiss Embassy in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to submit their complete application in person at the Swiss Embassy, Qatar. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.

## Visa Fees
Please contact the Swiss Embassy in Qatar directly in order to fix an appointment to submit your application form for a student visa.
Visa Fee: No Visa fees is applicable for applicants applying under student category.

## Documents Required
General requirements for applicants applying for Student visaPlease contact the Embassy of Switzerland in Qatar by mail (doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch (mailto:doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch)) in order to receive the information on the documentation to submit in order to apply for a “Student visa”.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa.

Click Here

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
Please contact the Swiss Embassy in Qatar directly in order to get the “Application Form D (Long Term visa) to submit your application for a student visa.

visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents Required

General requirements for applicants applying for Business visa

You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6th November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality of Liechtenstein.
Additional requirements
- Application form: The application form can be downloaded from this website. One form per person duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required. For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.
The form must be filled either in German, French, Italian or English as Arabic, Chinese, Russian etc. will be not accepted. Incomplete applications or not properly filled in and/or with missing or faulty documentation (including non-compliance to photo requirements) will be rejected.
Each application is checked individually and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued.
The Embassy cannot be held responsible for costs involved if a visa is refused.
- Photo: 2 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.
- Passport (signed original and one copy): The passport’s validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States. Please supply copies of obtained visas in the past.
Bear in mind that the passport remains at the Embassy during the processing time.
- Travel Insurance (copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30 000.
** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight tickets: Supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Tickets). Transportation details between the Schengen States
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or obvious and recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Bank statement: Bank statements of the last Six months.
- Non Qatari citizens:
- Original letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.
- Trade license (copy).
- Business trips:
- Invitation letter has to be submitted with the visa application or can be sent directly to the Swiss Embassy by fax (+974 4020 3402) or by mail (doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch (mailto:doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch)) from the host in Switzerland, including:
- Name and the passport number of the applicant.
- Reason of the business trip.
- Who will be bearing the cost for accommodation and medical expenses.
- Declaration that the applicant will return to Qatar before the visa expiry date.
Please note that additional documents might be requested.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Business]
Please note:
- There is a service charge of 101 Qatari riyals per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.
- The Visa fees can be paid in Qatari Riyals  at visa application centre. Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice. All fees are non-refundable.
- Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card/debit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation.
- Kindly have Confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.
- All fees paid towards VFS service charge and logistics are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid  at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid  at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Business]
Please note:
- There is a service charge of 101 Qatari riyals per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.
- The Visa fees can be paid in Qatari Riyals  at visa application centre. Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice. All fees are non-refundable.
- Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card/debit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation.
- Kindly have Confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.
- All fees paid towards VFS service charge and logistics are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Documents Required

General requirements for applicants applying for Business visa

You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6th November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality of Liechtenstein.
Additional requirements
- Application form: The application form can be downloaded from this website. One form per person duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required. For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.
The form must be filled either in German, French, Italian or English as Arabic, Chinese, Russian etc. will be not accepted. Incomplete applications or not properly filled in and/or with missing or faulty documentation (including non-compliance to photo requirements) will be rejected.
Each application is checked individually and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued.
The Embassy cannot be held responsible for costs involved if a visa is refused.
- Photo: 2 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.
- Passport (signed original and one copy): The passport’s validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States. Please supply copies of obtained visas in the past.
Bear in mind that the passport remains at the Embassy during the processing time.
- Travel Insurance (copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30 000.
** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight tickets: Supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Tickets). Transportation details between the Schengen States
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or obvious and recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Bank statement: Bank statements of the last Six months.
- Non Qatari citizens:
- Original letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.
- Trade license (copy).
- Business trips:
- Invitation letter has to be submitted with the visa application or can be sent directly to the Swiss Embassy by fax (+974 4020 3402) or by mail (doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch (mailto:doha.gcc@eda.admin.ch)) from the host in Switzerland, including:
- Name and the passport number of the applicant.
- Reason of the business trip.
- Who will be bearing the cost for accommodation and medical expenses.
- Declaration that the applicant will return to Qatar before the visa expiry date.
Please note that additional documents might be requested.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa.

Click Here

Photograph specifications, please click on link above

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
Click Here  (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/visumantragsformular.html)to download Visa Application Form

In case of domestic worker / s traveling along, below form will also have to be filled in by the sponsor in addition to the normal visa application forms / formalities

Passport Collection Authority letter

visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Checklist Visa for Tourism

NOC letter for minors 

You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6th November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality of Liechtenstein.
Additional requirements
- Application form: The application form can be downloaded from this website. One form per person duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required.
For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.
The form must be filled either in German, French, Italian or English as Arabic, Chinese, Russian etc. will be not accepted. Incomplete applications or not properly filled in and/or with missing or faulty documentation (including non-compliance to photo requirements) will be rejected.
Each application is checked individually and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued.
The Embassy cannot be held responsible for costs involved if a visa is refused.
- Photo:
2 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.
- Passport (signed original and one copy): The passport’s validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States. Please supply copies of obtained visas in the past.
Bear in mind that the passport remains at the Embassy during the processing time.
- Travel Insurance(copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30 000.
** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight tickets: Supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Tickets). Transportation details between the Schengen States.
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or obvious and recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Bank statement: Bank statements of the last Six months.
- Non Qatari citizens:
- Original letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.
- Trade license (copy).
- For Domestic / Household Employees applying:
- Liability Declaration (The form is available under the section “Download Forms”).
- Copy of the employer’s passport and valid Schengen visa.
- Have to be in Qatar at least one year and with the same sponsor.
For minors under the age of 18, traveling and/or applying without either one the parents/legal guardian, original Parental Authorization (Appendix 21.10) has to be submitted, duly signed by both their parents/legal guardian, together with copy of their passports
For students/school age children:
current school/university registration confirming that the student is exempted from attending lectures during the requesting travel period, with an original signature
Please note that additional documents might be requested.

visa_types.name: Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents Required

Checklist Visa for Visit

You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6 November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality of Liechtenstein.
Additional requirements
- Application form: The application form can be downloaded from this website. One form per person duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required.
For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.
For students/school age children:
current school/university registration confirming that the student is exempted from attending lectures during the requesting travel period, with an original signature
The form must be filled either in German, French, Italian or English as Arabic, Chinese, Russian etc. will be not accepted. Incomplete applications or not properly filled in and/or with missing or faulty documentation (including non-compliance to photo requirements) will be rejected.
Each application is checked individually and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued.
The Embassy cannot be held responsible for costs involved if a visa is refused.
- Photo: 2 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.
- Passport (signed original and one copy): The passport’s validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States. Please supply copies of obtained visas in the past.
Bear in mind that the passport remains at the Embassy during the processing time.
- Travel Insurance(copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30 000.
** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight tickets: Supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Tickets). Transportation details between the Schengen States.
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or obvious and recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Bank statement: Bank statements of the last Six months.
- Non Qatari citizens:
- Original letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.
- Trade license (copy).
- Proof of sufficient financial means: Bank statement (current and savings) for the last 6 months.)
- For Domestic / Household Employees applying:
- Liability Declaration (The form is available under the section “Download Forms”).
- Copy of the employer’s passport and valid Schengen visa.
- Have to be in Qatar at least one year and with the same sponsor.
Please note that additional documents might be requested.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Visit]
Please note:
- There is a service charge of 101 Qatari riyals per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.
- The Visa fees can be paid in Qatari Riyals  at visa application centre. Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice. All fees are non-refundable.
- Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card/debit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation.
- Kindly have Confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.
- All fees paid towards VFS service charge and logistics are non-refundable.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid  at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only Qatari nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Qatar can apply for a visa at the Switzerland Visa Application Centre in Qatar.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid  at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Visit]
Please note:
- There is a service charge of 101 Qatari riyals per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.
- The Visa fees can be paid in Qatari Riyals  at visa application centre. Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice. All fees are non-refundable.
- Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card/debit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation.
- Kindly have Confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.
- All fees paid towards VFS service charge and logistics are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Documents Required

Checklist Visa for Visit

You are eligible to apply for a visa at the Swiss Embassy if either the majority of your total stay is in Switzerland or if it equals the number of nights with a following Schengen country and it is the first destination on the tour.
In accordance with the agreement of 6 November, 1963 between Switzerland and the Principality of Liechtenstein, Swiss entry regulations also apply to applications to enter the Principality of Liechtenstein.
Additional requirements
- Application form: The application form can be downloaded from this website. One form per person duly completed, dated and signed by the applicant accompanied by the documentation required.
For applicants under the age of 18 years, both parents must sign the application form and have to join a copy of their signed passports.
For students/school age children:
current school/university registration confirming that the student is exempted from attending lectures during the requesting travel period, with an original signature
The form must be filled either in German, French, Italian or English as Arabic, Chinese, Russian etc. will be not accepted. Incomplete applications or not properly filled in and/or with missing or faulty documentation (including non-compliance to photo requirements) will be rejected.
Each application is checked individually and no guarantee can be given that a visa will be issued.
The Embassy cannot be held responsible for costs involved if a visa is refused.
- Photo: 2 recent passport-size photos of the applicant with white or light blue background.
- Passport (signed original and one copy): The passport’s validity shall extend at least three months after the intended date of departure from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, after the last intended date of departure from the territory of the Member States. Please supply copies of obtained visas in the past.
Bear in mind that the passport remains at the Embassy during the processing time.
- Travel Insurance(copy): Applicants for a visa shall prove that they are in possession of adequate and valid travel medical insurance to cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgent medical attention and/or emergency hospital treatment or death, during their stay(s) on the territory of the Member States. The insurance shall be valid throughout the territory of the Member States and cover the entire period of the person’s intended stay or transit. The minimum coverage shall be EUR 30 000.
** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.
- Flight tickets: Supply a copy of the electronic flight tickets (E-Tickets). Transportation details between the Schengen States.
- Hotel reservation (copy): Confirmed hotel reservation covering the entire duration of the stay, or obvious and recent proof of owning a house/flat in Switzerland.
- Bank statement: Bank statements of the last Six months.
- Non Qatari citizens:
- Original letter of your employer/sponsor duly stamped and signed, confirming your employment, position and salary.
- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.
- Trade license (copy).
- Proof of sufficient financial means: Bank statement (current and savings) for the last 6 months.)
- For Domestic / Household Employees applying:
- Liability Declaration (The form is available under the section “Download Forms”).
- Copy of the employer’s passport and valid Schengen visa.
- Have to be in Qatar at least one year and with the same sponsor.
Please note that additional documents might be requested.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa.

Click Here

Photograph specifications, please click on link above

## Download Form
Click Here  (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/visumantragsformular.html)to download Visa Application Form
In case of domestic worker / s traveling along, below form will also have to be filled in by the sponsor in addition to the normal visa application forms / formalities

Liability Declaration for household employees

Passport Collection Authority letter

** Travel health insurance has to be a local insurance or from Switzerland and the Insurance Certificate should be signed and stamped by the Insurance Company showing the dates, area of coverage and amount of coverage.

- Residency permit / ID card (copy): The end of the validity shall be at least three months prior of the intended date of return to Qatar from the territory of the Schengen Member States or, in the case of several visits, before the last intended date of return from the territory of the Member States.

- For Domestic / Household Employees applying:

- Copy of the employer’s passport and valid Schengen visa.

visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Household Employees]
Please note:
- There is a service charge of 101 Qatari riyals per application over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants.
- The Visa fees can be paid in Qatari Riyals  at visa application centre. Fees are subject to consular exchange rate changes without prior notice. All fees are non-refundable.
- Appointment/logistics fees must be paid online (with a credit card/debit card), kindly ensure that after completion of your appointment transaction/payment – you should receive Appointment confirmation.
- Kindly have Confirmation printed and submit the same upon submission of your application.
- All fees paid towards VFS service charge and logistics are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Photo Specifications
Please refer to attached document to know about acceptable photograph specifications as required to apply for Swiss visa.

Click Here

Photograph specifications, please click on link above

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Form
Click Here  (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/visumantragsformular.html)to download Visa Application Form
In case of domestic worker / s traveling along, below form will also have to be filled in by the sponsor in addition to the normal visa application forms / formalities

Liability Declaration for household employees

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
